content:
AI is automating repetitive coding, but developers who understand architecture, performance, accessibility, and user experience are becoming more valuable than ever.

4 min read
Just now

--

Eleven seconds.
That is how long it took AI to generate a login component that would have taken one of our junior developers nearly an hour.
We celebrated for about five minutes.
Then we discovered it failed on Safari, broke our accessibility checks, and would have increased backend costs every time users logged in.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

That was the moment I realized AI was not replacing web developers.
It was replacing the easiest part of web development.
The Work That Disappeared, and the Work That Did Not
AI tools are genuinely good at the part of web development that always felt like typing. Boilerplate components, CRUD scaffolding, basic form validation, converting a design mock into markup. That work is going away, and pretending otherwise wastes energy you could spend on the work that is not.
